description: Inside the vault, Sarge directs his team to search for P.E.G.s, dismissing valuables like diamonds as useless for their purpose. Jaco explains what they need to the saleswoman. Snowflake is kept from killing the hostage by Sarge. The urgency rises as the team prepares their strange device for escape, hinting at more advanced tech than S.H.I.E.L.D. is prepared for.

transcript.line: That won't be opening anytime soon.
Death is just the beginning of a magnificent journey.
You don't have to kill everything. We might need her.
Well, what are you all waiting for?
It's our fault that the other engineers are in trouble. We've got to help them.
Our options were limited. If the end goal is finding your friend Jemma in the future...
Yeah, end goal, sure, but I can't just sit here. Can you? Okay, cool, cool. Cool, cool, cool. Well, I would never be able to look Jemma in the eye again, so...
If we take action, we risk conflict with a superior force. I do not see how we can achieve all desired outcomes.
We find weapons, and we fight.
I am not that kind of Chronicom.
Well, then what? What?
I'm sorry to say, but if you plan on going against Viro, you may as well join the crew and step into the airlock yourself.
Suppose I don't have a choice.
Lock it down.
He's dead.
The lock's blown. So they're still in there?
No one's left the building.
Check the perimeter.
There's no other exits. If they want to leave, they'll have to get through us.
This all feels wrong.
I know. Why follow a terrorist attack with a jewel heist? What could they want?
Not sure. But no version of Coulson would come in here without an exit strategy. I need to see into that vault. How many people did Mack say went in there?
Three plus a hostage.
Then why do I see five heat signatures?
Still nothing?
They're all shiny but totally useless.
This place was supposed to have the good stuff. Where the hell is it?
Time's a factor now. Listen... “Dah-nah,” Snow here is dying to spill your spiritual energy all over the floor, but I can hold her off. Just need you to point us towards your premium merchandise.
You have hundreds of thousands of dollars' worth of diamonds right there. What more do you want?
This? You people care about this junk? I'm talking about P-E-Gs. You know, P-E-Gs. Help me, Jaco.
Piezoelectric gems. Crystals that are naturally polarized and can generate electric charge under applied mechanical pressure.
What? Crystals? We have some quartz... or topaz in the bottom drawer.
Finally. Thank you.
Keep looking. Better stock up while we can. Here. Let's see whether this one's a winner or not.
